title: runtime headless GitHub Actions validate verify
purpose: I3.2 defines runtime headless github actions validate verify for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools.
scope: Owns `I3.2` only; siblings under `I3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §11 — Branch I — Runtime & integration plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#11-branch-i-runtime-integration-plane).
behavior_steps: At H1 plan approval, the conductor stamps goal_id, optional parent_goal, and goal_type into state.json and the journal—the identity record every pursuit wake reloads before routing or verification.

On each wake, [A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) reads goal_type to bind contracts: milestone goals require manifest gates, feature goals bind task cards and evidence paths, and company_ops goals follow the ops queue.
When parent_goal is set, child pursuits inherit roll-up scope so parallel program streams report status to the parent without duplicating identity at each leaf or losing H3 roll-up.

The conductor and journal-keeper dual-write any H1 amendments to these fields; [B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) scripts refuse progression when goal_id collides or parent_goal points to a missing ancestor.

If identity drifts—duplicate ids, wrong parent—pursuit halts at H2, evidence must not attach to the wrong scope, and [A1.3](A1.3-goal-verify-command-meta-test.md) cannot pass until fields are reconciled.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.

acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: I3.2 defines runtime headless github actions validate verify for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools. In practice, the system at h1 plan approval, the conductor stamps goal_id, optional parent_goal, and goal_type into state.json and the journal—the identity record every pursuit wake reloads before routing or verification, then on each wake, [a2.1](a2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) reads goal_type to bind contracts: milestone goals require manifest gates, feature goals bind task cards and evidence paths, and company_ops goals follow the ops queue.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-i 2026-06-28 -->

GitHub Actions (or equivalent CI) runs validate-workflow.py and headless-verify on pull requests—deterministic gates before merge without human babysitting each test. CI does not clear H3 sign-off or design gates in state.json; it validates repo conformance and task evidence where configured.

Workflow definitions should mirror local verify commands from task cards and pack verify suites ([F1.8](F1.8-pack-verify-suites.md)). CI failure comments link to evidence logs; passing CI is necessary but not sufficient for goal_verify.
